front: Novel: Anna Wulf keeps four color-coded notebooks (black, red, yellow, blue); frame story 'Free Women'
Novel: Mary Turner murdered by her servant Moses
Novel: title from The Waste Land
Novel: set in Rhodesia
Author: semi-autobiographical Children of Violence series about Martha Quest in fictional Zambesia
Novel: Alice Mellings and the Communist Centre Union set off a car-bomb; she plays house mother to IRA-linked squatters
Novel: Anna wrote the autobiographical novel The Shadow of the Third and Frontiers of War about her time in Rhodesia
Novel: Tommy Portmain, Molly Jacobs's son, blinds himself in a failed suicide attempt; his father Richard is a business tycoon
Author: last novel of the Children of Violence quintet is The Four-Gated City, in which Martha Quest moves to London and witnesses World War III
Novel: five-book sci-fi series including Shikasta, The Sirian Experiments, and The Sentimental Agents in the Volyen Empire; features emissary Johor
Novel: the protagonist calls her German psychoanalyst 'Mother Sugar'
Novel: the protagonist exchanges opening line suggestions with her boarder Saul Green
Novel: described as 'soft' sci-fi emphasizing culture and society rather than futuristic technology
Novel: Mary strikes the tobacco farm worker Moses across the face with a sambok (whip), precipitating her murder
Short story: the boy Jerry swims under a rock formation and concludes 'it was no longer of the least importance to go to the bay'
Short story: Susan commits suicide by gas in a hotel room
Author: defended friend and Sufi teacher Idries Shah, who published a controversial translation of the Rubaiyat of Omar Khayyam
Author: wrote 'inner space fiction' inspired by Sufi mysticism, including Briefing for a Descent into Hell
Novel: in a dream, the protagonist sells a casket of ugly fragments to a crowd who transform into businessmen
Novel: a man takes away the protagonist's newspaper clippings about the hydrogen bomb test and the execution of the Rosenbergs
